title: Upon Scobble. Epig.
content:
Scobble for whoredom whips his wife; and cries
    He'll slit her nose; but blubb'ring, she replies,
    Good sir, make no more cuts i' th' outward skin,
    One slit's enough to let adultry in.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Upon Scobble. Epig."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
